role_summary: Private secretary at the NAACP national office (1948-1955); contributor to the Brown v. Board of Education legal work.
biographical_paragraph: Cecilia Suyat Marshall worked the NAACP's national office from 1948 to 1955 as private secretary to Dr. Gloster B. Current, the organization's national director, and on staff there she also took part in the legal work that became Brown v. Board of Education [src: 2]. She was born July 20, 1928 in Pu'unene, on Maui in what was then the Territory of Hawaii; her parents had emigrated from the Philippines in 1910 [src: 2]. In December 1955, the same year she stepped away from Current's office, she married Thurgood Marshall at St. Philip's Episcopal Church in Harlem; the marriage ran until Marshall's death in 1993 [src: 2]. She died on November 22, 2022 in Falls Church, Virginia, at ninety-four [src: 2]. Her oral history is held at the Library of Congress as entry 15 of the Civil Rights History Project [src: 1].

sources.title: Cecilia Suyat Marshall oral history
sources.publisher: Civil Rights History Project, Library of Congress
